comment: pacifist-bot band for --sim 5000 --policy pacifist (make sim, batch 5 T2, DECISION.md item 3 — the pacifist band gate mercy's fate hinges on). Measured 2026-07-19 with the T1 thresholds untouched (rat 2, goblin 3, ogre 4, see Monster::act_threshold in src/game.rs) and NO becalm light refund (the reserve knob was not needed): wins 1140/5000 (22.8%, win_pct 22 integer), deaths_combat 3841 (76.8%), deaths_dark 19 (0.38%), stuck 0, kills_total 0 (pacifist_never_attacks holds), spared_total 73345, median win turns 1319 (vs greedy's 1258 — becalming costs extra turns per encounter), median light left on win 355, min light left on win 3. Greedy control re-run same session, unchanged: wins 726/5000 (14.5%), deaths_combat 4266, deaths_dark 8, stuck 0 (tests/sim-band.json unmoved). FINDING (the old inversion hypothesis, measured not assumed): pacifist is NOT harder than greedy here — it wins MORE (22.8% vs 14.5%) and dies to combat LESS (3841 vs 4266 of 5000), because the stayed-swing rule (Game::try_act_player passes the ACTed monster to monsters_act as `stayed`, so it does not get to retaliate the turn it's talked to) removes a full turn of retaliation risk per encounter that a bump-attack does not — try_move_player's attack path calls monsters_act(None), so a monster that survives a hit can swing back immediately. The extra turns/light pacifist spends walking a monster down to calm (median win turns 1319 vs 1258, ~5% more) is real but smaller than the retaliation risk it avoids. deaths_dark is proportionally higher for pacifist (19 vs 8, both still a small minority of terminal outcomes) which is consistent with more turns burning more light, but combat is still the dominant death cause for both policies — no dark/combat minority inversion. Required shape per the batch-5 T2 brief: win_pct floor >= 5, cap <= 40 (mercy must be viable but must not dominate); this band is set with headroom around the measured 22 rather than pinned tight, matching tests/sim-band.json's precedent (its [10,25] brackets a measured 14.5 asymmetrically for the same reason: catch real drift, not day-to-day seed-set noise). Band is calibrated for 5000 seeds (smaller runs can trip the floor spuriously, same caveat as sim-band.json). Re-baselining (a knob change to regard thresholds or the becalm light refund) is a balance change: human sign-off required, same as tests/sim-band.json.
